_meta.contract_ref: CONTRACT.md §4 -- revenue path as IDENTITY in sourced drivers; ceiling check on every bound; no hand-drawn fade

global_semicon_rnd_pool.source: Top-20 semicon company R&D: $99B in 2024 (PatentPC/Yahoo Finance). Hyperscaler AI capex driving chip design R&D up 10-17% annually. Base: 10% CAGR. Bull: 13.6% (AI-driven design acceleration). Bear: 3.8% (cyclical slowdown + AI efficiency reducing per-chip R&D spend).

eda_ip_sim_wallet_share.base_argument: Modest expansion as agentic AI consumption adds a new metered layer on top of seat-based subscriptions. Historical level: 13-15%; targeting the upper end of historical range plus modest new consumption revenue.
eda_ip_sim_wallet_share.bull_argument: Consumption pricing breaks the historical ceiling -- AI agents invoke tools 3-5x more intensively than human engineers, and if 50% of that value is captured, wallet-share could reach 18%.
eda_ip_sim_wallet_share.bear_argument: AI productivity gains reduce engineering headcount faster than consumption pricing is adopted. Budget-constrained chip companies pass efficiency savings on to themselves, not to vendors. Wallet-share compresses toward 11-12%.
eda_ip_sim_wallet_share.note: The 13-15% current figure is from sell-side synthesis; no independent public confirmation. Flag as THEIA_BOTTOM_UP.

snps_share_of_combined_tam.base_argument: SNPS market share is stable in core EDA (duopoly equilibrium), modestly growing in simulation (Multiphysics Fusion unique offering) offset by IP China exposure. Blended share moves ~0.5pp over 4 years.
snps_share_of_combined_tam.bull_argument: Multiphysics Fusion captures 30-40% of the 3D IC signoff market where no competitor exists. Agent Engineer's breadth advantage over CDNS's depth approach gains wallet share in the AI-EDA transition. Total share reaches 34% vs current 31%.
snps_share_of_combined_tam.bear_argument: China EDA localization costs 2-3pp market share in Asia-Pacific. IP competition from ARM expansion and alternative interface IP vendors trims IP share. Ansys in simulation faces strengthened competition from Dassault/Hexagon as capital returns to those programs. Total share erodes to 28-29%.
snps_share_of_combined_tam.source: Multiple: Mordor Intelligence; MatrixBCG; SNPS revenue relative to EDA TAM; own calculations

driver_detail.eda_specific_wallet_share: EDA alone is ~8-9% of chip design R&D vs 13-15% for EDA+IP+Sim combined
driver_detail.secondary_driver: Agentic AI consumption adds a new revenue layer on top of market-share-based seat revenue; this is NOT captured in the wallet-share identity and is additive

actuals.source_note: SNPS reported two segments pre-Ansys: Design Automation and Design IP. FY2025 Design Automation estimated by applying ~53% revenue share to $7.054B total. Q2 FY2026 confirms Design Automation at 51.0% of revenue.

base.argument: EDA grows at market rate (8-10% CAGR) plus modest pricing power from duopoly position. Agent Engineer adoption adds ~0.5-1pp incremental growth annually from FY2028 as consumption pricing scales. China EDA revenue stable at 8-9% of segment (restricted primarily to IP, less to EDA tools proper). Backlog provides strong visibility through FY2026-FY2027.
base.ceiling_check: FY2030 EDA revenue of $7.09B vs EDA market growing to ~$18-20B by 2030 at 10-12% CAGR. Implied EDA share: $7.09B / $19B = 37.3%. This is above SNPS's current 31% share -- it implies either (a) SNPS gains share OR (b) the consumption pricing layer is additive to market share. Base case assumption: consumption pricing is additive (it is a new revenue stream, not market share). Excluding consumption, EDA share is ~31-32%. CEILING CHECK: PASSES if consumption is additive; would FAIL (require share gain) if consumption revenue is included in the TAM.
base.ceiling_note: FLAG: The EDA TAM definition from market research sources may not include consumption-based AI agent revenue, which is a new revenue category. If the $7.09B base case requires share expansion to 37% without consumption pricing, it is mildly aggressive. Recommend treating $6.5-7.0B as the base range with $7.5B as the bull ceiling.

implied_driver.interpretation: The identity implies SNPS holding ~57% of EDA wallet within the 8.5% EDA-specific wallet-share, consistent with a 31% SNPS share of the broader EDA market (which includes non-semiconductor applications and broader market definitions). Identity is internally consistent.

bull.argument: Agentic AI consumption pricing scales from 20 evaluation customers to 200+ by FY2028, adding a consumption revenue layer that is a fundamentally new billing category. Agent Engineer L4 orchestrates 25+ specialized agents; if average customer pays 1.5x their current subscription in new consumption charges, and SNPS has ~2,000 EDA customers, the incremental revenue from even 10% penetration of the base at 50% uplift is material. Concurrently, the AI chip design boom (each hyperscaler maintaining 5+ ASIC programs, each requiring a full EDA stack) expands the customer count from ~1,500-2,000 to 2,500-3,000 as startups, regional cloud providers, and automotive OEMs start chip design programs. Bull assumes 14-16% EDA market CAGR vs base 10-12%.
bull.ceiling_check: $8.80B / EDA market $22B (at 12% CAGR to 2030) = 40% EDA share. If consumption revenue is NOT included in the TAM, SNPS maintains 31% market share and the $8.80B requires the TAM to be $28B (EDA market growing 16% CAGR). A 16% CAGR for a $12B market is aggressive -- bull sits at the edge of credibility on TAM assumptions. FLAG: Citi's concern about backlog lagging peers is relevant here. Bull requires new business generation to ACCELERATE, not just continue.
bull.ceiling_flag: NEAR_CEILING -- bull assumes either (a) consumption revenue adds to the TAM, or (b) EDA market grows faster than consensus (16% vs 12% CAGR). Both are plausible but each is a high reading of its respective assumption.

bear.argument: Agentic AI compresses engineering headcount 20-30% by FY2029. SNPS's seat-based revenue compresses proportionally. Consumption pricing adoption is slower than expected -- large customers (hyperscalers) negotiate ELAs (enterprise license agreements) with capped consumption charges. China EDA revenue declines 40-50% from current levels as Empyrean and Primarius capture trailing-edge design starts. New export restrictions reimposed. Net: EDA grows only 2-3% above inflation, barely covering price increases on renewals.
bear.ceiling_check: $5.50B / EDA market $15-16B (at 6% CAGR in bear scenario) = ~35% EDA share. Bear is internally consistent -- SNPS actually holds or slightly gains EDA share but the market grows slowly. PASSES ceiling check.

driver_detail.design_starts_driver: Number of new chip design programs (hyperscaler, fabless, IDM) requiring interface IP blocks (PCIe, HBM, UCIe, CXL, LPDDR)
driver_detail.ip_spend_per_design: Varies by complexity; leading-edge AI chips may use $30-50M of IP per design (multiple interface standards); legacy chips $2-10M
driver_detail.royalty_stream: Emerging; hyperscaler royalty model transition near end FY26. Royalties are per-chip-shipped, not per-design-start
driver_detail.snps_ip_market_share: ~55% in wired interface IP (the relevant sub-segment); ~13-22% overall semiconductor IP

actuals.source_note: IP declined in FY2025 due to China disruptions and export restrictions May-July 2025. Q2 FY2026: $454M (20% of revenue), up 12% QoQ from Q1's ~$405M. FY2026 full-year estimated at $1.9-2.0B (Theia bottom-up from quarterly data; GS/Citi guide $1.8B).

base.argument: IP recovers from the FY26 transitional trough (China headwinds, model transition) and returns to mid-teens growth. Drivers: (1) UCIe and advanced chiplet interconnect IP are required in every multi-die design -- this is a structural demand pull, not cyclical; (2) HBM interface IP is in every AI accelerator; Nvidia H-series, B-series, AMD Instinct all use HBM3/3E requiring SNPS HBM PHY IP; (3) PCIe 7.0 IP launched (industry first per SNPS) -- every server platform refresh requires new PCIe IP; (4) Royalty model transition adds recurring streams from FY2028 as designs complete their 1-2 year cycles. FY2027 may see a one-time revenue recognition headwind (~$100-200M) from the royalty model transition (upfront fees deferred to royalty streams) before royalty volumes compensate in FY2028.
base.transition_headwind_note: FY2027 IP may be below the linear trend due to royalty model transition recognition timing. Daedalus should model FY2027 IP at $2.0-2.1B (slightly below the trend line) before accelerating to $2.5-2.6B in FY2028 as royalties ramp.
base.ceiling_check: $3.38B / IP TAM ($8.5B growing to ~$13B at 11% CAGR to 2030) = 26% share. SNPS currently at 13-22% overall; 26% is achievable if wired interface IP (SNPS's strength at 55%+ share) becomes a larger fraction of the total IP TAM as chiplet/multi-die proliferates and processor IP (ARM-dominated) grows slower than interface IP. CEILING CHECK: PASSES.

bull.argument: Royalty model transition succeeds: Google, AWS, Microsoft, Meta all adopt royalty-bearing SNPS IP terms. At hyperscaler AI chip volumes (Google deploying millions of TPU v5 chips, AWS billions of Trainium inference chips), even $0.05-0.15/chip royalty creates a $200-500M recurring annual revenue stream by FY2029-FY2030. This is ON TOP of base design-start-driven IP revenue. Additionally, UCIe as the emerging chiplet interconnect standard positions SNPS as the gatekeeper for the multi-die ecosystem -- every participating chiplet needs UCIe IP, and SNPS is a founding member and the IP leader in this standard.
bull.ceiling_check: $4.30B / IP TAM ~$14B (at 13% CAGR bull) = 30.7%. This requires SNPS to gain significant IP share from ~22% to ~31%. Achievable only if: (a) wired interface IP grows FASTER than the overall IP market AND (b) SNPS's 55% wired interface IP share is maintained. Both are plausible but together represent the upper range. FLAG: Sits at the top of the credible range -- not impossible but requires everything to go right in IP.

bear.argument: China remains structurally impaired and declines from ~10% to ~5% of IP segment over 4 years. Chinese companies adopt domestic IP (Empyrean's analog IP is already competitive at trailing nodes). The royalty model transition for hyperscalers hits customer resistance -- Amazon and Google have teams specifically built to avoid royalty-bearing IP architectures (the same reason they moved away from ARM processor designs in some contexts). FY2027 headwind from model transition is not offset by royalty ramp until FY2028-FY2029, leaving IP essentially flat for two years. ARM expands into interface IP through new licensing models, fragmenting SNPS's 55% wired interface IP share.
bear.ceiling_check: $2.20B / IP TAM ~$11B (at 6% CAGR bear) = 20% share. In line with current levels. PASSES -- no impossible share assumption required.

segment_revenue_paths.driver_identity: Ansys_revenue = Existing_ACV × renewal_rate + New_ACV_bookings × conversion_to_revenue + cross_sell_to_EDA_customers

driver_detail.existing_acv: $2.3B+ annual contract value at acquisition close, growing 10-12% on constant currency
driver_detail.renewal_rate: High recurring revenue; Ansys historically 90%+ annual renewal rates on maintenance
driver_detail.cross_sell: Multiphysics Fusion cross-sell to existing SNPS EDA customers is the new channel; this did not exist pre-acquisition
driver_detail.chip_to_system_new_market: Semiconductor customers who use SNPS EDA and now need chip-to-system thermal/EM simulation for AI hardware

actuals.fy2025_note: Ansys closed July 17, 2025. SNPS FY2025 ends October 31. So ~3.5 months of contribution = $757M.

segment_revenue_paths.fy2026_note: Q2 FY2026 Ansys segment $652M. Annualizing H1 ($652M + Q1 implied ~$670M based on $9.665B full-year guide less other segments) suggests ~$2.7-2.9B FY2026. Management guiding $2.9B at midpoint. Use $2.77B as Theia's estimate (between H1 annualized and management guide, acknowledging typical H2 seasonality uplift).

base.argument: Ansys's base business (automotive, aerospace, industrial simulation) grows at 10-12% CAGR consistent with its pre-acquisition growth rate ($2.545B FY2024, up 12%). Cross-sell to EDA customers adds 1-2pp incremental growth from FY2028 as Multiphysics Fusion scales. Cost synergies ($400M by Year 3) support margin expansion but do not directly drive revenue. The automotive electrification and robotics/physical AI tailwinds are secular and not dependent on SNPS execution -- they drive Ansys's existing customer base growth.
base.ceiling_check: $4.25B / engineering simulation TAM (~$5-7B at 13% CAGR to 2030 for engineering physics simulation specifically) = 61-85%. FLAG: This is a tight ceiling test. If the relevant TAM is only ~$5B by 2030, $4.25B implies ~85% share -- which is impossible (no product has 85% share in a competitive market). Resolution: the TAM is WIDER than pure engineering simulation. Ansys addresses broader simulation categories (systems, embedded software, digital twins) that the narrow 'multiphysics simulation software' TAM figures miss. Management's $31B combined TAM implies Ansys's addressable market is $18-20B (combined TAM minus EDA minus IP). At $4.25B revenue vs $18-20B TAM, Ansys captures ~21-24% of its addressable market. CEILING CHECK: PASSES on the broader TAM definition.
base.ceiling_resolution_note: The narrow 'multiphysics simulation software' TAM ($300-450M from Mordor) grossly understates Ansys's addressable market. Ansys at $2.5B FY2024 revenue already dwarfs this narrow TAM, confirming the narrow figure is mis-scoped. The relevant TAM is broader: ALL engineering simulation including FEA, CFD, electromagnetics, systems -- estimated at $8-15B. This is the source of TAM measurement divergence across reports.

bull.argument: Multiphysics Fusion adoption by AI hardware companies accelerates beyond base case. Every major AI chip program by FY2027-FY2028 requires chip-to-system thermal/electromagnetic co-simulation as 3D IC complexity makes manual simulation workflows inadequate. If NVIDIA, AMD, Broadcom, and 5+ hyperscalers all adopt Multiphysics Fusion at $10-20M per program per year, and SNPS has 20-30 such programs running simultaneously, incremental revenue is $200-600M. The physical AI (autonomous vehicles, industrial robots) design wave also accelerates, pulling through system-level simulation spending that Ansys is uniquely positioned to capture.
bull.ceiling_check: $5.10B / broader Ansys TAM $22B (at 14% bull CAGR) = 23.2%. PASSES -- well below ceiling.

bear.argument: Ansys integration is slower than expected. The cross-sell motion (EDA team selling simulation to mechanical engineers) faces organizational friction -- different personas, different champion relationships, different budget holders. The $400M revenue synergy pushes from Year 4 to Year 6-7. Ansys's core market (automotive, aerospace) faces cyclical headwinds as electric vehicle adoption slows in 2026-2027 (observable trend) and aerospace capital programs are delayed. Dassault and Hexagon (MSC) respond aggressively with pricing to defend their accounts, compressing Ansys's pricing power. Growth decelerates from 12% to 3% as synergy realization fails.
bear.ceiling_check: $3.10B / Ansys TAM $11B (at 8% bear CAGR) = 28.2%. PASSES -- Ansys is already a large-share incumbent.

segment_revenue_paths.note: Processor IP Solutions divestiture pending per management. Power Artist already divested. Segment becomes de minimis by FY2027. Not modeled separately in Daedalus's SOTP.

total_revenue_paths.note: Total = EDA + Design IP + Ansys + Other

base.implied_total_check: FY2030 $14.72B vs SNPS-stated combined TAM of $31B (base growing to ~$38B by 2030 at 11% CAGR). SNPS captures 39% of TAM. From current 31%, this requires share gain or TAM expansion. The driver: Multiphysics Fusion consumption revenue is additive to the existing TAM, and IP royalty streams may add to measured revenue without proportional TAM growth in traditional market research databases.
base.ceiling_check: CONDITIONAL PASS -- requires some combination of share gain and revenue-category expansion (consumption, royalties). Not a free lunch; depends on execution.
base.note_on_management_guidance: Management FY2026 guidance midpoint $9.665B. No published FY2027+ guidance. GS estimates FY2026 organic revenue ~$9.5B (ex-divestitures) growing ~9.5% organically. Citi estimates similar. Base case $10.71B FY2027 implies ~11% YoY growth from FY2026 actuals -- slightly ahead of organic rate, reflecting Ansys contribution ramp and IP recovery from transitional year.

bull.ceiling_check: $18.20B vs combined TAM ~$42B (at 13% CAGR to 2030). SNPS capture: 43%. Current: 31%. A 12pp share gain in 4 years requires every differentiated initiative (agentic AI, Multiphysics Fusion, IP royalty) to simultaneously succeed at scale. FLAG: APPROACHING CEILING. The bull is credible if consumption pricing and Multiphysics Fusion are additive to the TAM; it is at or above ceiling if they merely redistribute existing TAM spending. Treat as the TRUE UPSIDE case, not a realistic central estimate.

bear.ceiling_check: $10.80B vs TAM ~$32B (at 7% bear CAGR). SNPS capture: 33.8%. In line with current 31% -- bear scenario is stagnation, not collapse. PASSES.

non_gaap_operating_margin_paths.note: For Daedalus SOTP: per-segment margin inputs, not just blended. These are NON-GAAP adjusted operating margins.

fy2026_actual.ansys_pct: Not separately disclosed; blended non-GAAP 39.5% implies Ansys ~28-32% on current revenue mix
fy2026_actual.source: Q2 FY2026 10-Q Design Automation adjusted margin 43.3%; Design IP 24.4%; blended non-GAAP 39.5%

base.argument: EDA margin expands to 47% from 43% as AI agent consumption revenue is high-incremental-margin. IP margin recovers to ~32% as royalty streams add near-pure-profit revenue. Ansys margin converges toward EDA margin as $400M cost synergies fully deliver and the combined business achieves operating leverage. Blended margin reaches 42-43% range, consistent with GS's normalized operating margin assumptions.

bull.argument: Consumption pricing for agentic AI has near-zero marginal cost -- each additional $1 of agent-driven revenue flows almost entirely to operating income. If $500M+ of agentic consumption revenue develops by FY2029-FY2030, the incremental margin on that revenue layer is 80-90%. This pulls blended margins toward 47-50%.

bear.argument: Cost synergies underdelivered ($200M vs $400M target). IP margin compressed by China revenue loss (higher-margin business lost). Ansys faces integration costs that persist longer than expected. R&D intensity must increase to compete with CDNS on agentic AI (counter-programming required to maintain L4/L5 parity). Blended margin flat to slightly contracting despite revenue growth.

assumption_ladder.note: Per-case numbered assumption ladder for Daedalus's scenario matrix

base.row: Implied pool requirement (FY2030 revenue / wallet-share / SNPS-share)
base.value: Pool $145B × wallet 15% × share 31.5% = $6.9B (plus ~$7.8B from Ansys non-semicon TAM) = ~$14.7B -- CHECK PASSES

base.row: SNPS share of combined TAM FY2030

base.value: ~$8B (assuming $3B annual FCF applied to debt reduction)
~200M (modest dilution from SBC net of buybacks; debt priority for now)
$14.72B × 42.5% = $6.26B adj. operating income; after tax at 13% effective = ~$5.4B NOPAT

bull.value: TAM ~$42B × 43% capture = $18.1B -- AT CEILING. Assumes consumption/royalty is additive to existing TAM.
Agentic AI consumption $500M+; IP royalty $300-500M; Multiphysics Fusion $200-400M -- all three must deliver

bear.value: Pool $115B × wallet 11.5% × share 28.5% = $3.8B semicon-derived + Ansys $3.1B base = $6.9B (bear implies semicon TAM-derived revenue of $3.8B vs actuals of $6.7B for EDA+IP combined, which requires dramatic share compression). Rechecking: EDA+IP bear = $5.50B + $2.20B = $7.70B. At pool $115B × wallet 11.5% × SNPS share this implies SNPS is getting 58% of the EDA+IP wallet -- internally consistent only if the pool growth is slow AND wallet share contracts.
AI headcount compression -25%, China IP -40%, Ansys cross-sell fails, royalty transition delays FY2027 IP
~$10B (slower FCF, slower debt repayment due to margin compression)

monitoring_variables.note: Daedalus and subsequent agents: watch these quarterly to update the scenario probabilities

variables.watch: Is it stabilizing or continuing to decline? Two more quarters of decline would signal the bear's pipeline slowdown thesis is materializing.

variables.variable: Design IP QoQ revenue growth in 2H FY2026
variables.current: Q2 IP was up 12% QoQ from Q1
variables.watch: Management guided IP recovery in 2H. Q3 and Q4 FY2026 IP revenue should sustain QoQ growth. If it reverses, the royalty model transition is creating a larger revenue hole than expected.

variables.variable: Agent Engineer customer count and consumption revenue disclosure
variables.current: 20 customers evaluating 25+ agents (as of Converge March 2026)
variables.watch: First-time disclosure of consumption revenue bookings or contribution would de-risk the bull scenario. Watch for commentary in Q3/Q4 FY2026 earnings.

variables.current: 50% of $400M cost synergies expected by end FY2026
variables.watch: Non-GAAP operating margin trajectory for the combined entity. If blended margin expands from 39.5% toward 41-42% by Q4 FY2026, cost synergies are on track.
New US export restrictions are the tail event. Organic localization (Empyrean share gains) is the base case drag. Stabilization at 8-9% would indicate the floor has been found.

variables.current: GA launched June 17, 2026. Beta customers described as 'market leaders' -- not named
variables.watch: Customer naming of Multiphysics Fusion programs in earnings commentary (AMD, NVIDIA, Intel Foundry, hyperscalers). First named wins would validate the cross-sell thesis.
variables.frequency: Quarterly (first win expected Q4 FY2026 or Q1 FY2027)

sources.name: Synopsys Q2 FY2026 earnings transcript (Globe and Mail)
Futurum Group SNPS Q2 FY2026 revenue breakdown
Coherent Market Insights simulation software $15.46B market 2026
SemiAnalysis hyperscaler custom silicon $15-20B new design activity (cited by multiple secondary sources)
